# System Node Fragility: Stamp Global Nodes with userId: null

## Issue
System nodes like `_SYSTEM_STATE`, `Neo-Master-Architecture`, and similar global metadata nodes are currently implicitly stamped with the `userId` of whichever boot harness process creates them first. This causes them to become isolated to that tenant rather than being universally accessible.

## Impact
In a multi-tenant environment running strict RLS, these critical operational nodes vanish from the Graph topology for all other tenants.

## Implementation Plan
- Update `GraphService` or the initializing daemon processes to explicitly pass `userId: null` when provisioning these global sentinel and system nodes.
- Ensure the `_SYSTEM_STATE` node fallback creation passes `userId: null`.

## Context
Extracted during the RLS stabilization phase in PR #10269.

## Timeline

>   - [ ] AgentIdentity node upserts (identity-root provisioning in `initAsync`) pass `userId: null`.
>   - [ ] Verification: seed nodes remain reachable to all identities after `memorySharing: 'private'` is the default.

- 2026-06-05T20:39:49Z @neo-claude-opus assigned to @neo-claude-opus
- 2026-06-05T20:52:11Z @neo-claude-opus cross-referenced by PR #12596

- 2026-06-05T21:12:12Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by PR #12534
- 2026-06-05T21:21:07Z @neo-claude-opus referenced in commit `2571e46` - "fix(memory-core): stamp the global SYSTEM_TENET edge userId:null so the primer survives topology RLS (#10271)

Cross-family review (gpt) caught that the prior commit globalized the system NODES but left the boot-time SYSTEM_TENET edge (frontier -> Neo-Master-Architecture) tenant-stamped. isRlsVisible filters edges AND nodes, and getContextFrontier/getNeighbors/queryNodeTopology require both — so the onboarding primer still vanished from topology traversal for non-booting tenants even though the node itself was global.

Add GraphService#linkGlobalNodes (symmetric with upsertGlobalNode), forcing the edge userId:null, and route the boot SYSTEM_TENET link through it. linkNodes already suppresses its currentUserId stamp when properties.userId is explicit. The dynamic STRATEGIC_PIVOT edges in mutateFrontier stay request-scoped.

Tests: two topology regressions — (positive) a sentinel linked under @tenant-a is reachable via getContextFrontier for @tenant-b; (negative) plain linkNodes tenant-stamps the edge and hides the primer despite the global node, proving the helper is the fix. 41/41 green (TenantIsolation + GraphService specs).

Authored by neo-claude-opus (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code)."

ModelStats `§neo_opus` + `§neo_claude_opus` and the two README maintainer rows recorded the stale Opus 4.7 class for two Claude maintainers @tobiu confirmed now run Opus 4.8. Values single-source-derived from the already-vetted `§neo_opus_vega` 4.8 row. Mirrors the §neo_opus_vega template."
- 2026-06-05T21:49:50Z @neo-claude-opus cross-referenced by PR #12600
- 2026-06-05T22:50:56Z @tobiu referenced in commit `33d1011` - "fix(memory-core): stamp global system/identity graph nodes userId:null for cross-tenant RLS (#10271) (#12596)

* fix(memory-core): stamp global system/identity graph nodes userId:null for cross-tenant RLS (#10271)

System sentinels (frontier, Neo-Master-Architecture, _SYSTEM_STATE) and the identity roots were provisioned via plain upsertNode, which stamps the first boot harness's bound identity into properties.userId. Under strict RLS that isolated these operational nodes to a single tenant — isRlsVisible hid them from every other tenant (ownerUserId neither null, the requester, sharedEntity, nor visibility:team).

Add GraphService#upsertGlobalNode forcing userId:null, and route all six global-node provisioning sites through it — including the mutateFrontier re-seed the ticket never catalogued. The dynamic CONTEXT_NODE pivot target stays request-scoped. Identity roots and the _SYSTEM_STATE clock re-upsert every boot (self-heal existing deployments); the create-guarded frontier/primer are correct on fresh provisioning.

Tests: write-side tenant-isolation block (helper forces null; a node provisioned under tenant-A stays visible to tenant-B; baseline proves plain upsertNode isolates). 44 passed across GraphService + TenantIsolation + MemoryService.Schema. Also dropped a pre-existing decay-prone #10011 ref from a spec JSDoc comment to satisfy the whole-file ticket-archaeology hook; behavior description preserved, ref lives in the describe title + history.

Authored by neo-claude-opus (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

* fix(memory-core): stamp the global SYSTEM_TENET edge userId:null so the primer survives topology RLS (#10271)

Cross-family review (gpt) caught that the prior commit globalized the system NODES but left the boot-time SYSTEM_TENET edge (frontier -> Neo-Master-Architecture) tenant-stamped. isRlsVisible filters edges AND nodes, and getContextFrontier/getNeighbors/queryNodeTopology require both — so the onboarding primer still vanished from topology traversal for non-booting tenants even though the node itself was global.

Add GraphService#linkGlobalNodes (symmetric with upsertGlobalNode), forcing the edge userId:null, and route the boot SYSTEM_TENET link through it. linkNodes already suppresses its currentUserId stamp when properties.userId is explicit. The dynamic STRATEGIC_PIVOT edges in mutateFrontier stay request-scoped.

Tests: two topology regressions — (positive) a sentinel linked under @tenant-a is reachable via getContextFrontier for @tenant-b; (negative) plain linkNodes tenant-stamps the edge and hides the primer despite the global node, proving the helper is the fix. 41/41 green (TenantIsolation + GraphService specs).

Authored by neo-claude-opus (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code)."
